title: Diplacrum laevissimum
taxonomy: Diplacrum laevissimum is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Diplacrum, which is a group of grass-like plants. The plant is native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in Fiji and Samoa. Diplacrum laevissimum is classified as a type of sedge, which is a common name for plants in the Cyperaceae family. Further classification includes its order (Poales), class (Liliopsida), and division (Angiospermophyta).
